body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 53/2026
Made under: Residential Tenancy Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: April 7, 2026

B.C. Reg. 53/2026, deposited April 7, 2026, under theRESIDENTIAL TENANCY ACT[section 97]. Order in Council 130/2026, approved and ordered April 7, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Residential Tenancy Regulation, B.C. Reg. 477/2003, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix. — C. BOYLE,Minister of Housing and Municipal Affairs; R. PARMAR,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	The Residential Tenancy Regulation, B.C. Reg. 477/2003, is amended by adding the following heading before section 1: Division 1 – Definitions and Interpretation. (a)	in subsection (1) by adding the following definition: "specified agreement", in relation to a rental unit, means a housing agreement under section 483[housing agreements for affordable housing and special needs housing]of theLocal Government Actor section 565.2[housing agreements for affordable housing and special needs housing]of theVancouver Charter, if the terms and conditions of the housing agreement that apply to the rental unit restrict (a)	the availability of the rental unit to certain classes of persons identified in the agreement, and (b)	the rent, or the formula for calculating the rent, that may be charged for the rental unit;, and (b)	by adding the following subsections: (3)	For the purposes of section 6 (3) (b)[unenforceable term]of the Act, a term of a tenancy agreement is "unconscionable" if the term is oppressive or grossly unfair to one party. (4)	For the purposes of the definition of"public housing body"in section 49.1 (1)[landlord’s notice: tenant ceases to qualify for rental unit]of the Act, the following persons and organizations are prescribed: (a)	the British Columbia Housing Management Commission; (b)	the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation; (d)	the City of Vancouver Public Housing Corporation; (e)	the Metro Vancouver Housing Corporation; (f)	the Capital Region Housing Corporation; (g)	any housing society or non-profit municipal housing corporation that has an agreement regarding the operation of residential property with the following: (i)	the government of British Columbia; (ii)	the British Columbia Housing Management Commission;

minutes_briefs.title: BC clarifies 'unconscionable' tenancy terms and adds housing agreement rules
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has amended the Residential Tenancy Regulation to add a definition for 'specified agreement' — a housing agreement that restricts who can rent a unit and how much rent can be charged. The amendment also clarifies that a tenancy term is 'unconscionable' (oppressive or grossly unfair to one party) and lists specific public housing bodies, including BC Housing and CMHC, for the purpose of landlord notices when a tenant no longer qualifies for a rental unit.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review your tenancy agreements for any terms that could be considered oppressive or grossly unfair to tenants.

key_dates.label: BC Residential Tenancy Regulation amendment takes effect
